Article.ArticleTitle: Comparative genomics reveals evolutionary signatures of genes associated with jumping in beetles.

Abstract.AbstractText: Jumping has independently emerged as an effective escape strategy across multiple beetle lineages (order Coleoptera). Characterization of jumping behavior not only informs the development of bioinspired robotic systems but also advances understanding of adaptive processes and convergent evolution. However, despite extensive behavioral studies in beetles, the genetic mechanisms and evolutionary trajectories underlying this locomotor strategy remain largely unresolved. To elucidate the molecular basis of jumping adaptation and convergent evolution, a comparative genomic analysis was conducted using high-quality assemblies, including one newly generated genome, from jumping beetles representing three families and their non-jumping sister taxa spanning over 200 million years of evolutionary divergence. Genes associated with energy metabolism exhibited extensive signals of rapid evolution and positive selection in jumping beetles, consistent with the elevated energy requirements of explosive locomotion. These observations parallel previous reports linking energy metabolism genes to other high-demand locomotor modes such as flight, suggesting shared molecular signatures across functionally distinct behaviors. Jumping beetles exhibited convergent amino acid substitutions in bab1, a regulator of leg disc development, alongside evidence of positive selection and accelerated evolution in the dynein gene Dnai4 and a significant expansion in copy number of the skeletal muscle gene Fhl2. Together, these results implicate both limb morphogenesis and muscle performance genes in the emergence of beetle jumping. This study highlights key genetic mechanisms underlying behavioral innovation and offers novel insights into adaptive convergence in insect locomotion.

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 跳跃行为是节肢动物逃避危险的有效策略。在鞘翅目甲虫中，不同的类群独立演化出跳跃行为。跳跃行为对于仿生学应用以及动物的适应性进化和趋同进化具有重要的研究意义。但是，尽管已有大量研究探讨了甲虫的跳跃行为，这一行为模式背后的遗传基础还不清楚。为了研究这一问题，我们首先组装了黄栌胫跳甲的染色体水平基因组，然后结合已有的高质量基因组数据对跳跃甲虫、近缘的非跳跃甲虫进行比较基因组分析（覆盖3科和2亿多年的进化历史）。分析结果表明跳跃甲虫的能量代谢相关基因经历了显著的加速进化和/或正选择，反映了跳跃行为高度消耗能量这一本质。该结果与早期研究提出的能量代谢基因参与了动物飞行等行为的观点一致，暗示不同行为模式背后很可能存在共有的分子进化特征。我们的结果还表明，跳跃甲虫的腿部发育基因 bab1发生了显著的趋同进化，动力蛋白基因 Dnai4同时经历了正选择和加速进化，骨骼肌基因 Fhl2则出现了显著的基因拷贝数扩张。这些发现支持腿和肌肉相关基因很可能在甲虫跳跃行为的演化过程中发挥了重要作用。总之，该研究深化了对甲虫跳跃行为遗传适应和趋同演化的理解和认识。.
